summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #245 (ContextMenu agnostic core) and #249 (MenuBar agnostic core) in one combined PR targeting fogodev/ars-ui, following the repo's TDD, spec-conformance, proptest, mutation, and post-implementation-audit workflow.
summary.outcome: Both modules were fully implemented, reviewed by Codex across six review passes (addressing 12 findings), all behavior tests and proptests pass, specs were updated, and the PR (#679) is open with Codex approval—but the CI branch-coverage ratchet kept failing due to a mismatch between local crate-only coverage and the CI merged-LCOV denominator; the final coverage fix commit was pushed but its CI result is unconfirmed.

repo: Snapshot tests follow a per-part/per-branch naming convention—one `.snap` file per named anatomy part or output-affecting state branch—not one aggregate snapshot per component.
The post-implementation audit skill requires at least two 'anything else missing?' rounds and coverage audit across unit, snapshot, proptest, mutation, spec-conformance, llvm-cov, and doc coverage.
The waiting-for-codex-review loop uses PR issue reactions as state: 👀 = reviewing in progress, 👍 = approved; all unresolved Codex threads must be addressed before the next @codex review trigger.

The `cargo xtask coverage check` ratchet runs against a merged workspace LCOV artifact produced by CI, whose branch denominator is significantly larger than a crate-local `cargo llvm-cov nextest -p ars-components` run—local crate coverage can pass while CI fails.

The `cargo mutants` baseline invocation does not pass `--features i18n`; any test file that conditionally compiles helpers under that feature must be gated consistently, or the baseline compile fails before any mutant runs.
The agnostic core pattern uses `NoEffect` as the machine's effect type; all platform side effects (live focus, positioning, dismissal timers) belong in framework adapters, not the core machine.
Direction::Auto must be resolved from the stored locale at dispatch-helper call time, not stored as Auto in the resolved direction field used for arrow-key routing.
`ContextOpen` must reset transient submenu and typeahead state when re-opening an already-open context menu.
Disabling a component mid-session must synchronize all dependent state fields (open/active, highlight, typeahead, callbacks) in a single SyncProps transition, not just flip the disabled flag.
MenuBar must always maintain a valid roving-tabindex entry trigger (one trigger with tabindex=0) while enabled—both after UpdateMenus and after re-enable via SyncProps.

code.finding: The highlight proptest helpers were not gated under #[cfg(feature = "i18n")], causing cargo mutants baseline compile failure even though the module only compiles meaningfully with that feature enabled.

The trigger keydown ArrowDown activation arm was originally guarded with `dir == Ltr`, breaking RTL horizontal menubars; the arm must be unconditional because ArrowDown means 'open menu' regardless of text direction.
The adjacent_key helper loop-wrap branch (line 733) was consistently uncovered; exercising it requires starting from the actual last item in the collection, not a middle item.
The action_plan close-via-item-selection branch was uncovered; on_open_change fires when an action item selection closes the menu, which requires a test that dispatches SelectItem in the open state.

The existing menu module is the canonical template for new selection core machines: item types, typeahead delegation, disabled behavior, StaticCollection, ComponentIds, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, and proptest invariant shape.

workflow: Running local `cargo llvm-cov nextest -p <crate> --branch` is necessary but not sufficient to verify CI coverage—the CI gate runs against a merged workspace LCOV whose branch denominator includes integration and feature-flag test runs; download the CI coverage artifact with `gh run download --name coverage-diagnostics` and run the xtask check against that file before committing a coverage fix.

Adding assertion-heavy test functions can lower branch percentage even when they exercise product code, because test control flow adds branches to the denominator; prefer extending existing test functions with a few extra calls over adding new standalone functions.

cargo mutants can stall in the final `cargo test --no-run` compile step when other cargo jobs are running concurrently; SIGKILL the process and rely on previously-completed per-file passes rather than waiting indefinitely.
When a CI job produces a flaky failure unrelated to the branch changes, `gh run rerun --failed` restarts only the failed jobs without re-running the entire workflow.
The repo's pre-push gate is `cargo xci-fast`; running it locally before the first push catches clippy, fmt, spec, snapshot, and integration issues that would otherwise surface as CI failures.
Spec files in spec/components and spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components must all be updated consistently when an agnostic-core contract changes, not just the primary component spec.

friction: Local crate branch coverage consistently reported passing (90.0%+) while CI merged-LCOV kept failing by 2–7 branches, requiring four separate coverage-fix commits and multiple full CI runs before the gap was diagnosed.
cargo mutants silently stalled in the final compile phase for over 30 minutes before being force-killed; it produced no error output and appeared to be making progress.

The CI coverage artifact download (`gh run download --name coverage-diagnostics`) was not attempted until after three failed coverage-fix commits; discovering this tool earlier would have saved two full CI run cycles.
A flaky e2e test (ars-e2e::fixtures::fixture_server_wait_reports_timeout_output_and_removes_log) failed a CI run unrelated to the selection changes, consuming a full CI cycle before diagnosis.

Codex posted findings across six review passes (12 total threads); while all were valid, the review loop took longer than expected because each @codex review trigger required waiting through a full CI fan-out before Codex began reviewing.
The nightly rustfmt formatter disagreed with stable fmt on the split match arms in the RTL ArrowDown fix, requiring an additional format-fix commit.
open_items: Final CI Coverage ratchet result for the last commit (covering context menu ratchet gaps) is unconfirmed in the transcript—PR #679 must be verified green before merge.
Adapter components for ContextMenu and MenuBar (Leptos and Dioxus) are explicitly out of scope for this PR and remain unimplemented.
cargo mutants was run once against context_menu and menu_bar at initial implementation; the post-review behavioral fixes were not re-verified by mutation testing due to the stuck mutants process.
The spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components adapter specs received automated text replacements but were not validated against actual adapter implementations.
PR #679 requires issues #245 and #249 to auto-close via the 'Closes' keywords upon merge—verify both issues close correctly after merge.
